user.title: Connect to openHAB
user.description: Enter the address of your openHAB server and an API token. Create a token in openHAB under Settings → API Tokens.

data_description.base_url: The address of your openHAB server, including the scheme and port

reauth_confirm.title: openHAB rejected the API token
reauth_confirm.description: Enter a new API token for {url}.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach openHAB at that address.

abort.already_configured: That openHAB server is already configured.
abort.reauth_successful: Reconnected to openHAB with the new token.

init.title: openHAB Bridge
init.description: Manage the openHAB items exposed to Home Assistant.

add_items.title: Add openHAB items
add_items.description: Choose the items to expose as Home Assistant entities. Only items that are not already exposed are listed.

add_types.title: Choose entity types
add_types.description: Each item is pre-set to the closest matching entity type. Change any you would like to expose differently.

add_advanced.title: Advanced (optional)
add_advanced.description: Device class, state class and unit are normally derived automatically from the openHAB item type. Leave any of these on “Auto” to keep that; only set one if you need something different.

edit_item.title: Edit an item
edit_item.description: Choose which exposed item to change.

edit_details.title: Edit {item}
edit_details.description: openHAB type: {item_type}. The label in openHAB is “{label}”. Leave the name override empty to keep using the openHAB label.

edit_advanced.title: Advanced: {item}
edit_advanced.description: Device class, state class and unit are normally derived automatically from the openHAB item type. Leave any of these on “Auto” to keep that.

remove_items.title: Remove items
remove_items.description: Selected items will stop being exposed and their entities will be removed.

connection.title: Connection settings
connection.description: Change how Home Assistant reaches openHAB. Your exposed items are left untouched.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach openHAB at that address.

abort.cannot_connect: Could not reach openHAB to load the item list.
abort.no_items_available: Every openHAB item is already exposed.

init.title: openHAB item “{item}” is gone
init.description: This item is no longer present in openHAB. If it was renamed, re-map it so the Home Assistant entity keeps its history and stays working in your automations. Otherwise, remove it.

remap.title: Re-map “{item}”
remap.description: Search for the openHAB item that replaces it, by name or label. Narrow the search until it matches {limit} items or fewer.

remap_pick.title: Replace “{item}” with
remap_pick.description: The existing entity keeps its ID and history, so automations and dashboards carry on working.

error.no_matches: No unexposed openHAB item matches that. Try part of the item name or its label.
error.too_many_matches: Too many matches to show. Try a more specific search.

abort.entry_gone: That openHAB Bridge entry no longer exists.
abort.cannot_connect: Could not reach openHAB to load the item list.

init.title: “{item}” changed type
init.description: The item is now a {item_type} in openHAB, which the current entity type can no longer represent. Choose an entity type that fits.

abort.entry_gone: That openHAB Bridge entry no longer exists.

parse_failures.title: openHAB item “{item}” is sending unusable values

init.title: “{item}” is sending unusable values
init.description: This item keeps sending values the current entity type cannot interpret, such as “{value}”. Choose an entity type that fits.

abort.entry_gone: That openHAB Bridge entry no longer exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach openHAB
unreachable.description: Home Assistant has been unable to reconnect to openHAB for some time. The last error was: {error}. Entities stay unavailable until the connection is restored; this will clear on its own once it is.

websocket_unsupported.title: This openHAB has no event WebSocket
websocket_unsupported.description: The server at {entry_title} does not provide the /ws/events endpoint this integration needs for live updates. openHAB 4.0 or newer is required. There is no polling fallback.

feedback_loop.title: Feedback loop on openHAB item “{item}”
feedback_loop.description: This item was commanded repeatedly in a short window, which normally means something is commanding it every time it changes — usually a Home Assistant automation or an openHAB rule. Outbound commands to this item are paused for a short period; incoming state from openHAB is unaffected. Review your automations and rules for “{item}”.

commands_unconfirmed.title: openHAB item “{item}” is not acting on commands
commands_unconfirmed.description: Commands sent to “{item}” are not resulting in any state change. This item has autoupdate disabled in openHAB, so its state only changes when the bound thing reports back — the thing is most likely offline or rejecting the command. This is not a fault in the connection to openHAB, and will clear once a command takes effect.

get_item_state.name: Get item state
get_item_state.description: Reads the current state of any openHAB item, whether or not it is exposed as an entity.

config_entry_id.name: openHAB server
config_entry_id.description: Which openHAB server to query. Optional when only one is configured.

post_update.name: Post update
post_update.description: Writes an item state directly in openHAB, without asking the bound thing to act.

config_entry_id.name: openHAB server
config_entry_id.description: Which openHAB server to use. Optional when only one is configured.

state.name: State
state.description: The new state, for example ON or 21.5.

send_command.name: Send command
send_command.description: Sends a command to an openHAB item, asking the bound thing to act. For items with autoupdate disabled the state only changes once the thing reports back.

config_entry_id.name: openHAB server
config_entry_id.description: Which openHAB server to use. Optional when only one is configured.

command.name: Command
command.description: The command to send, for example ON, OFF or 50.
